title: Extraction Protocol for Untargeted LC-MS/MS from Microbial Cultures
author: Mauricio Caraballo

steps.title: Prepare metadata table according to ReDU2-MS2...
steps.instruction: Prepare metadata table according to ReDU2-MS2 Sample Information Template [for more information about the ReDu, see the Preprint from our lab: Repository-scale Co- and Re-analysis of Tandem Mass Spectrometry Data as well as the ReDu platform ].

steps.title: Fill the metadata table with all the applicable information
steps.instruction: Fill the metadata table with all the applicable information. Information from LC-MS/MS can be included later on after data has been acquired.

steps.title: Additional columns can be created to...
steps.instruction: Additional columns can be created to include information related to the specificity of the experiments (e.g. microbial interactions: co-cultures vs monocultures).

steps.title: Specifically, from each sample we will...
steps.instruction: Specifically, from each sample we will need at least the NCBITaxonomy (e.g. 1313|Streptococcus pneumoniae ).

steps.title: Exometabolome
steps.instruction: Exometabolome : extraction procedure performed on supernatant from microbial cultures [See Pinu & Villas-Boas, 2017 ]. Briefly, for liquid cultures :

steps.title: For semi-solid cultures (eg
steps.instruction: For semi-solid cultures (eg. containing agar), the following protocol can be used: If the culture is performed using 96-deep well plates, then the extraction can be performed using a 96-well SPE plate.

steps.title: Add 1 mL methanol and sonicate ( 00:15:00 )
steps.instruction: Add 1 mL methanol and sonicate ( 00:15:00 ).

steps.title: Centrifuge ( 865 x g, °C,...
steps.instruction: Centrifuge ( 865 x g, °C, 15 minutes ) to separate cell pellets from supernatant

steps.title: Transfer the supernatant to a new...
steps.instruction: Transfer the supernatant to a new 96-well plate and store at -80 °C if the extraction will not be performed immediately. Also, if the cell pellets will be extracted separately, they can be stored at -80 °C until extraction.

steps.title: Prepare the 96-well SPE plate before performing the extraction
steps.instruction: Prepare the 96-well SPE plate before performing the extraction. See the SPE steps described below.

steps.title: The following are the steps for SPE
steps.instruction: The following are the steps for SPE : Specific protocols can vary from providers, so follow recommendations from the providers of the purchased product: Phenomenex, Sigma-Aldrich or Waters. Briefly, the steps are:

steps.title: Now the extracts ( 1 mL...
steps.instruction: Now the extracts ( 1 mL of methanol containing the extracted metabolites) are in a 96-deep well plate.

steps.title: You can dry them under vacuum
steps.instruction: You can dry them under vacuum. Dried samples will be suitable for shipping to the Dorrestein Lab. Endometabolome: extraction procedure performed on filtered microbial cells (pellets) [For optional protocols, such as for B. subtilis, see Meyer et al., 2013 . This review Pinu et al., 2017 might be also helpful];

steps.title: Include, together with the samples, blanks...
steps.instruction: Include, together with the samples, blanks of the culture media used in each experiment (extractedunder exact conditions as the exometabolome).

steps.title: If possible, use Deepwell Plates, 96-well, Polypropylene, 2000µL [e
steps.instruction: If possible, use Deepwell Plates, 96-well, Polypropylene, 2000µL [e.g. E951033600 ]. Include the mapof the samples’ name/label.

steps.title: If shipping standards together with the...
steps.instruction: If shipping standards together with the microbial extracts is possible, please include them as well astheir information in the metadata table.

steps.title: At least biological replicates (3-6) from each sample
steps.instruction: At least biological replicates (3-6) from each sample. We run technical replicates for each sample.Ship dry extracts if possible.

steps.title: Separate microbial culture from cells either by centrifugation or filtration
steps.instruction: Separate microbial culture from cells either by centrifugation or filtration.

steps.title: Add internal standard to monitor extraction...
steps.instruction: Add internal standard to monitor extraction procedure (although it can be optional, the addition of a known compound will be useful to guarantee the efficiency of the extraction procedure).

steps.title: Quenching (deactivate enzymatic activity) by freeze-drying...
steps.instruction: Quenching (deactivate enzymatic activity) by freeze-drying or any other drying process (such as vacuum-drying): at this step, samples can be shipped to us as they are dry;

steps.title: If not quenching but direct extraction...
steps.instruction: If not quenching but direct extraction is performed, it can be done via liquid-liquid extraction or SPE (solid-phase extraction) [the extracted metabolites will depend on the sorbent material used in SPE].

steps.title: Condition the cartridge with methanol ( 2 mL )
steps.instruction: Condition the cartridge with methanol ( 2 mL ).

steps.title: Equilibrate with a similar solvent as the sample
steps.instruction: Equilibrate with a similar solvent as the sample. In this case, water ( 2 mL ) as the supernatant is aqueous.

steps.title: Load the sample ( 1 mL...
steps.instruction: Load the sample ( 1 mL of supernatant is fine for a 100mg SPE cartridge).

steps.title: Wash the sample ( 1 mL of water)
steps.instruction: Wash the sample ( 1 mL of water).

steps.title: Elute ( 1 mL of methanol)
steps.instruction: Elute ( 1 mL of methanol).

steps.title: Optional
steps.instruction: Optional: recondition cartridges for another SPE.

steps.title: After the incubation period freeze/thaw the...
steps.instruction: After the incubation period freeze/thaw the plate (10-15mins, 3 times) to lyse the cells. After the incubation period, freeze/thaw the plate ( 00:10:00 - 00:15:00 ) to lyse the cells (1/3). After the incubation period, freeze/thaw the plate ( 00:10:00 - 00:15:00 ) to lyse the cells (2/3). After the incubation period, freeze/thaw the plate ( 00:10:00 - 00:15:00 ) to lyse the cells (3/3).
